channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026: Guaranteed Internship, Global Career Path, and Hands-On Training in Europe
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026, organized by Pixel in Florence, Italy, is a career-focused postgraduate programme designed to equip graduates and young professionals with the practical skills, global exposure, and professional experience needed to succeed in international project management roles. This unique master’s programme stands out for its guaranteed internship […]

The post International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026: Guaranteed Internship, Global Career Path, and Hands-On Training in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026, organized by Pixel in Florence, Italy, is a career-focused postgraduate programme designed to equip graduates and young professionals with the practical skills, global exposure, and professional experience needed to succeed in international project management roles.

This unique master’s programme stands out for its guaranteed internship component, strong employment outcomes, and hands-on approach to learning. With over 250 alumni from more than 40 countries and an 85% employment rate in international project roles, the programme has established itself as a leading pathway into careers in European and global cooperation projects.

About the Master Programme

The Master in European Project Planning and Management is a specialized training programme aimed at preparing participants for careers in international development, European Union-funded projects, and global cooperation initiatives.

The programme integrates:

  • Academic learning
  • Practical project simulations
  • Real-world internship experience

It is designed to bridge the gap between academic education and professional employment by focusing on applied skills and direct industry exposure.

Why Choose This Master Programme

The programme offers a number of distinctive advantages that make it highly competitive and career-oriented.

Key Reasons to Apply

  1. Guaranteed International Internship
    • All participants receive a 3-month internship placement
    • Internships available in over 60 organizations across 20+ European countries
    • Roles typically include Assistant European Project Manager
  2. Strong Career Outcomes
    • 85% of alumni are employed in international project roles
    • Graduates work across Europe and globally
  3. Hands-On Learning Approach
    • Real project simulations
    • Case studies and applied learning
    • Practical exposure to EU funding systems
  4. International Learning Environment
    • Participants from over 40 countries
    • Global network of students and professionals
  5. Expert Faculty
    • Trainers with 25–30 years of experience
    • Professionals in project planning, evaluation, and auditing
  6. Institutional Expertise
    • Organized by Pixel, with over 27 years of experience in European projects and international training

Programme Structure

The Master programme is divided into two main modules, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of both project planning and management.

Module 1: European Project Planning

This module focuses on developing strong project design and proposal skills.

Topics Covered

  • European policies and funding opportunities
  • Identifying and matching ideas with funding programmes
  • Understanding calls for proposals
  • Project idea development
  • Transnational partnership creation
  • Budget planning and financial development
  • Application form preparation
  • Project quality and evaluation criteria

Module 2: European Project Management

This module focuses on implementing and managing projects effectively.

Topics Covered

  • Project management cycle
  • Contractual procedures and amendments
  • Time management and planning tools
  • Logical Framework Approach
  • Risk management and quality assurance
  • Leadership and communication
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Financial and administrative management
  • Reporting and documentation

Internship Programme

A key feature of the Master is its guaranteed internship component.

Internship Details

  • Duration: 3 months
  • Placement: 60+ partner organizations
  • Countries: 20+ European countries
  • Roles: Assistant European Project Manager

Internship Formats

  • Onsite internships in Europe
  • Online internships for flexible participation

Internships are tailored to each participant’s profile, ensuring alignment with career goals and interests.

Skills Acquired

By the end of the programme, participants will gain a wide range of professional competencies.

Core Skills

  • Project planning and proposal development
  • Budget preparation and financial management
  • Partnership coordination
  • Project implementation and monitoring
  • Reporting and evaluation
  • Communication and leadership

Career Opportunities

Graduates of the programme go on to work in a variety of roles and sectors.

Career Roles

  • European Project Manager
  • Project Officer
  • International Cooperation Officer
  • Research Project Manager

Employment Sectors

  • Universities and research centres
  • NGOs and international organizations
  • Public institutions and EU bodies
  • Private companies and consultancies

Programme Format and Duration

Key Details

  • Duration: 5 months (800 hours)
  • Training Phase: 2 months
  • Internship Phase: 3 months
  • Language: English

Study Options

Participants can choose between:

  1. Onsite Programme
    • Location: Florence, Italy
    • Immersive international experience
  2. Online Programme
    • Flexible learning format
    • Access to recordings and tutor support

Start Dates

  • Synchronous (Onsite/Online): Mid-October
  • Asynchronous Online: Available anytime

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to:

  • Recent graduates
  • Young professionals
  • International applicants

Requirements

  • University degree or diploma
  • Good command of English

Tuition Fees

  • Online Programme: €2,900
  • Onsite Programme: €3,800

About Pixel

Pixel is an international education and training institution based in Florence, Italy, with extensive experience in:

  • European project planning
  • International cooperation
  • Higher education training

Participants benefit from Pixel’s strong network and expertise in EU-funded projects.

How to Apply

Applicants can:

  • Book a consultation call
  • Apply through the Official Website

Application Link

Apply for the Master Programme

Why You Should Apply

This programme is ideal for individuals seeking to:

  • Build a career in international project management
  • Gain hands-on experience in European projects
  • Access global job opportunities
  • Develop practical and transferable skills
  • Join a global professional network

Click HERE to read more about the International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026.

Visit OFY for more other global Internship and Scholarship opportunities.

The post International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026: Guaranteed Internship, Global Career Path, and Hands-On Training in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Catalyst Academy 2026: Youth Social Innovation Program in Africa (Apply Now)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Catalyst Academy is a regional youth social innovation learning program implemented across Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Cameroon, and South Africa. Led by entrepreneurs and academics from Catalyst Now, the program is designed to equip young people with the skills, networks, and opportunities needed to thrive as social innovators. Through peer learning circles, technology-enabled platforms, and […]

The post Catalyst Academy 2026: Youth Social Innovation Program in Africa (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Catalyst Academy is a regional youth social innovation learning program implemented across Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Cameroon, and South Africa. Led by entrepreneurs and academics from Catalyst Now, the program is designed to equip young people with the skills, networks, and opportunities needed to thrive as social innovators.

Through peer learning circles, technology-enabled platforms, and cross-sector collaboration, the program engages youth participants in co-creating solutions to pressing community challenges. Participants gain access to mentorship from experienced social innovators across Africa and beyond, alongside a structured learning journey that includes facilitated sessions, mentorship, and continuous peer exchange.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to:

  • Young aspiring social entrepreneurs aged 18–35 years
  • Individuals interested in social innovation
  • Young women facing barriers to entrepreneurship
  • Youth, especially persons living with disabilities or refugees/displaced persons seeking dignified employment opportunities
  • Community leaders aiming to scale their social impact

Applicants must be nationals or residents of one of the following countries:
Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Cameroon, or South Africa.

Program Structure and Support

The Catalyst Academy is designed to provide both direct support to participants and wider community impact through knowledge sharing and innovation.

Direct Participant Support Includes:

  • Weekly peer learning sessions to build knowledge, skills, and confidence
  • Connection to experienced mentors in relevant fields
  • Full access to a digital learning platform and curated resources
  • Opportunities to participate in cross-border online learning exchanges
  • Entry into the Catalyst Now network and alumni community

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the program will:

  • Build a real-world project addressing a community challenge
  • Collaborate in small teams with other young changemakers
  • Learn through hands-on, experiential learning rather than theory alone
  • Develop and test a pilot solution rather than only conceptual ideas

Application Note

Applicants are encouraged to carefully complete all sections of the application, as the form includes multiple detailed questions.

Candidates should provide authentic and personal responses. The application is an opportunity for selection teams to understand each applicant’s motivations, experiences, and ideas, so generic or AI-generated responses should be avoided.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

Deadline

The deadline to submit applications is 20 April 2026.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post Catalyst Academy 2026: Youth Social Innovation Program in Africa (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Now Open: CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Leaders in Urban Planning & Sustainability
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
If you’re passionate about shaping stronger communities while building a meaningful career in public service, the CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to do both. This award-winning program connects emerging leaders with local governments and community organizations to tackle pressing community planning challenges. Fellow Applications Deadline: May 22 at 5 PM PT What is […]

The post Now Open: CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Leaders in Urban Planning & Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re passionate about shaping stronger communities while building a meaningful career in public service, the CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to do both. This award-winning program connects emerging leaders with local governments and community organizations to tackle pressing community planning challenges.

  • Fellow Applications Deadline: May 22 at 5 PM PT

What is CivicSpark?

CivicSpark is an evidence-based fellowship program designed to strengthen local workforce capacity while advancing critical community planning priorities. Through hands-on service, fellows gain real-world experience and contribute to impactful projects that improve local communities.

About the Fellowship

Each year, CivicSpark recruits over 100 emerging leaders eager to develop professional skills and make a difference. During the 11-month service term, fellows:

  • Conduct research and data analysis
  • Support planning and policy development
  • Implement community-based projects
  • Collaborate with local governments and organizations

Fellows work on initiatives such as:

  • Waste management systems
  • Urban and regional planning
  • Sustainability and environmental programs
  • Community engagement strategies

Why Join CivicSpark?

For Fellows

  • Gain hands-on experience in public sector work
  • Build a strong professional network
  • Develop leadership and project management skills
  • Make a measurable impact in local communities

For Host Organizations

  • Receive dedicated support on community planning projects
  • Increase the likelihood of achieving project goals
  • Build internal capacity and workforce development

Organizations that partner with CivicSpark are 5 to 15 times more likely to achieve their project goals compared to those without a fellow.

CivicSpark Impact at a Glance

  • 12 Years of building local workforce capacity
  • 1.25 million hours of service delivered
  • 1,700 community events hosted
  • 110,000+ community members engaged
  • 300 local public agencies supported
  • 1,000 emerging leaders trained
  • 73% of alumni continue working in the public sector
  • 95%+ of projects meet or exceed expectations
  • Active in California, Colorado, and Washington, with expansion opportunities

Awards & Recognition

CivicSpark’s impact has been widely recognized, including:

  • 2024 Segal AmeriCorps Alums Segal Leadership Award
  • 2020 Governor’s Office of Business & Economic Development – ZEVe Award
  • 2018 Governor’s Volunteering & Service Award (Agency of the Year)
  • 2017 Service Year Impact Award (Mid-Size Program)

How to Apply

Become a Fellow

If you’re ready to launch your career while making a difference, apply to become a CivicSpark Fellow before May 22 at 5 PM PT.

Click Here, on the application proces

For more details about this Fellowship, click Here

Host a Fellow

Organizations seeking support for community planning projects can apply to host a fellow by May 14.

Whether you’re an aspiring civic leader or a public agency looking to amplify your impact, CivicSpark offers a proven pathway to success. By bridging talent with community needs, the program continues to transform both careers and communities.

Apply now and be part of a movement driving real change.

Discover more similar Fellowship opportunities here.

The post Now Open: CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Leaders in Urban Planning & Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNDP Nigeria GEF Small Grants Programme 2026 (OP8): Fully Funded Opportunity for NGOs and Community Organizations to Secure Up to $50,000 for Environmental Projects
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are a civil society organization, grassroots group, or development-focused institution seeking funding to drive real environmental impact, the Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme (GEF-SGP) Operational Phase 8 (OP8) under the United Nations Development Programme presents a powerful opportunity. About the UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme (OP8) The GEF Small Grants Programme (SGP) […]

The post UNDP Nigeria GEF Small Grants Programme 2026 (OP8): Fully Funded Opportunity for NGOs and Community Organizations to Secure Up to $50,000 for Environmental Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a civil society organization, grassroots group, or development-focused institution seeking funding to drive real environmental impact, the Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme (GEF-SGP) Operational Phase 8 (OP8) under the United Nations Development Programme presents a powerful opportunity.

About the UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme (OP8)

The GEF Small Grants Programme (SGP) is a flagship global initiative funded by the Global Environment Facility and implemented by UNDP. Since its inception, it has supported over 26,000 community-led projects worldwide focused on environmental sustainability and poverty reduction.

The Operational Phase 8 (OP8) builds on decades of impact by expanding support for:

  • Grassroots innovation
  • Community resilience
  • Inclusive development (especially women, youth, and marginalized groups)
  • Scalable environmental solutions

At its core, the programme empowers local actors to lead sustainable development efforts within their communities.

Funding Value and Project Scope

Successful applicants can receive:

  • Grants typically ranging from $5,000 to $50,000 per project
  • Additional technical support and capacity-building assistance
  • Opportunities to scale impact through partnerships and networks

Projects usually run for 1–3 years, depending on scope and design.

Focus Areas of the Grant

The OP8 funding call prioritizes innovative projects in the following thematic areas:

  1. Biodiversity Conservation
  2. Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation
  3. Land Degradation and Sustainable Land Use
  4. Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems
  5. Low-Carbon Energy Access
  6. Chemicals and Waste Management
  7. Sustainable Urban Solutions

These focus areas align with global environmental priorities while addressing local community needs.

Who Can Apply (Eligible Applicants)

This opportunity is specifically designed for non-profit and community-driven entities, including:

  • Civil Society Organizations (CSOs)
  • Community-Based Organizations (CBOs)
  • Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs)
  • Grassroots groups and local associations
  • Academic and research institutions
  • Foundations and cooperatives

Applicants must be legally registered and demonstrate strong community engagement capacity.

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • This specific call is hosted by UNDP Nigeria, meaning it primarily targets organizations operating within Nigeria.
  • However, similar OP8 opportunities are being implemented across multiple developing countries globally, especially in Africa, Asia, and Latin America

Target Beneficiaries and Priority Groups

The programme strongly emphasizes inclusion and prioritizes projects that benefit:

  • Women and youth
  • Indigenous peoples and local communities
  • Vulnerable and marginalized populations
  • Rural and underserved communities

This reflects the programme’s mission to combine environmental sustainability with social equity.

Key Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be a registered non-profit organization
  • Demonstrate experience in community-based projects
  • Align project ideas with GEF-SGP strategic priorities
  • Show clear environmental and social impact potential
  • Provide evidence of community participation and ownership
  • Demonstrate organizational and financial capacity

Projects must also include:

  • Defined objectives and outcomes
  • Budget (including co-financing contributions)
  • Implementation strategy
  • Measurable impact indicators

How to Apply (Step-by-Step Process)

The application process is structured in two major stages:

1. Submit a Project Concept Note

Applicants begin by developing a brief concept paper outlining:

  • Project idea
  • Objectives
  • Expected outcomes
  • Budget estimate

2. Screening and Review

  • The concept is reviewed by the National Coordinator
  • Eligible concepts are shortlisted

3. Full Proposal Development

Shortlisted applicants are invited to submit a detailed project proposal.

4. Final Evaluation

  • Reviewed by a National Steering Committee
  • Selected projects receive funding

Apply here

Selection Criteria

Projects are evaluated based on:

  • Alignment with environmental priorities
  • Innovation and scalability
  • Community ownership and participation
  • Inclusion of women, youth, and vulnerable groups
  • Cost-effectiveness and sustainability
  • Potential for measurable impact

Why You Should Apply

This is not just a funding opportunity—it is a global platform for impact.

By securing a GEF-SGP grant, your organization will:

  • Gain international recognition
  • Access technical expertise and partnerships
  • Strengthen institutional capacity
  • Scale local solutions to global challenges

Most importantly, you will contribute to sustainable development while improving livelihoods at the community level.

Deadline

14th April 2026

The UNDP Nigeria GEF-SGP Operational Phase 8 Call for Project Concepts is one of the most impactful grant opportunities available for grassroots organizations in the environmental and development sectors.mWith strong funding support, global backing, and a clear focus on community empowerment, this programme offers a rare chance to transform innovative ideas into real, measurable change.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post UNDP Nigeria GEF Small Grants Programme 2026 (OP8): Fully Funded Opportunity for NGOs and Community Organizations to Secure Up to $50,000 for Environmental Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN JPO Programme 2025/2026: Entry-Level International Jobs for Developing Countries and Italy Nationals
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme for 2025/2026 is now open, offering a wide range of career opportunities across leading international organizations. These positions provide early-career professionals with the chance to gain hands-on experience in global development, humanitarian work, policy, and international cooperation. This year’s intake includes positions for candidates from developing countries as well […]

The post UN JPO Programme 2025/2026: Entry-Level International Jobs for Developing Countries and Italy Nationals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme for 2025/2026 is now open, offering a wide range of career opportunities across leading international organizations. These positions provide early-career professionals with the chance to gain hands-on experience in global development, humanitarian work, policy, and international cooperation.

This year’s intake includes positions for candidates from developing countries as well as a large number of roles reserved for candidates with Italian nationality.

Positions for Candidates from Developing Countries (2 Openings)

A limited number of highly competitive positions are available for applicants from eligible developing countries:

  • JPO26-12 – IFAD (Rome, Italy)
    Partnership Analyst (Member State Engagement)
  • JPO26-30 – UNDP (Lilongwe, Malawi)
    Human Rights Analyst

Eligible Nationalities

Applicants must be nationals of one of the following countries:

Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, Angola, Armenia, Bangladesh, Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cambodia, Central African Republic, Chad, Colombia, Comoros, Côte d’Ivoire, Cuba, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Djibouti, Egypt, El Salvador, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Gabon, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Haiti, Iraq, Jordan, Kenya, Kyrgyzstan, Kiribati, Lao PDR, Lebanon, Lesotho, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Malawi, Mali, Mauritania, Moldova, Morocco, Mozambique, Myanmar, Nepal, Niger, Pakistan, Palestine, Republic of the Congo, Rwanda, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Solomon Islands, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Tajikistan, Tanzania, Timor-Leste, Togo, Tunisia, Tuvalu, Ukraine, Uganda, Yemen, Zambia.

Positions for Candidates with Italian Nationality (45 Openings)

A broad selection of JPO roles is available across multiple organizations and global locations. These include:

Selected Highlights

  • Alliance Bioversity and CIAT (Accra, Ghana / Rome, Italy / Abidjan, Côte d’Ivoire)
    Project Officers – Sustainable Cocoa Initiative
  • FAO (Rome, Italy / Abidjan, Côte d’Ivoire)
    Digital Agrifood Systems Specialist; Food Standards (CODEX Secretariat)
  • IFAD (Abidjan, Côte d’Ivoire)
    Regional Technical Analyst (Markets and Value Chains)
  • ILO (Amman, Jordan)
    Employment Creation and Development Cooperation
  • IOM (Addis Ababa, Ethiopia / Nairobi, Kenya)
    Migration Health Policy Officer; Innovation Officer (Climate Action)
  • OECD (Paris, France)
    Policy Analyst; Junior Economist
  • UNDP (New York, USA)
    Partnerships Development Analyst
  • UNEP (Nairobi, Kenya)
    Associate Intergovernmental Affairs Officer
  • UN-Habitat (Nairobi, Kenya)
    Urban and Regional Planning for Sustainable Development
  • UNICEF (Amman, Jordan / Dakar, Senegal)
    Adolescent Development Officer; Gender Programme Officer
  • UNIDO (Vienna, Austria)
    Agribusiness Development; Human Resource Management
  • UNODC (Abidjan, Côte d’Ivoire)
    Drug Control and Crime Prevention (Border Management)
  • WFP (Addis Ababa, Ethiopia)
    Procurement Officer (Supply Chain Unit)
  • World Bank (Washington DC, USA)
    Education Reform, Performance and Learning (GPE Secretariat)

Additional roles are available across institutions such as EBRD, IAEA, UNESCO, UNFPA, UNHCR, UNOPS, UNOV, and more—bringing the total to 45 positions.

Why Apply for the JPO Programme?

The JPO Programme is a prestigious pathway into international careers, offering:

  • Professional experience with top global organizations
  • Exposure to international development and policy work
  • Opportunities to work in diverse, multicultural environments
  • Career progression within the UN system and partner institutions

How to Apply

Interested candidates should review the specific requirements for each position and submit their applications through the official JPO programme channels or the respective organization’s careers portal.

Click Here, for details of each application

Pro Tip for Applicants

Given the competitiveness of JPO roles, tailor your application carefully:

  • Highlight relevant international experience
  • Demonstrate alignment with the organization’s mission
  • Emphasize technical expertise and language skills

Early preparation and strong, targeted applications significantly increase your chances of success.

Final Thoughts

The JPO 2025/2026 cycle presents an exceptional opportunity for young professionals to launch or advance their careers in international development and diplomacy. With roles spanning continents and sectors, this programme remains one of the most sought-after entry points into the global development ecosystem

Discover more similar Job opportunity Here. 

The post UN JPO Programme 2025/2026: Entry-Level International Jobs for Developing Countries and Italy Nationals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AAI 2026 Applications Open: Join the ARROW–AMwA Feminist Advocacy Institute (Fully Funded)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The ARROW – AMwA Advocacy Institute 2026 (AAI 2026) is now accepting applications from young feminist leaders across the Global South. This dynamic program is designed to equip the next generation of advocates with the knowledge, tools, and networks needed to drive transformative change in their communities and beyond. Organized by the Asian-Pacific Resource and […]

The post AAI 2026 Applications Open: Join the ARROW–AMwA Feminist Advocacy Institute (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ARROW – AMwA Advocacy Institute 2026 (AAI 2026) is now accepting applications from young feminist leaders across the Global South. This dynamic program is designed to equip the next generation of advocates with the knowledge, tools, and networks needed to drive transformative change in their communities and beyond.

Organized by the Asian-Pacific Resource and Research Centre for Women and Akina Mama wa Afrika, the institute builds on years of impactful initiatives supporting feminist leadership, advocacy, and movement-building across Africa, Asia, and the Pacific.

About the Advocacy Institute

The ARROW – AMwA Advocacy Institute (AAI) aims to strengthen Global South solidarity and empower young women to lead advocacy efforts on critical social justice issues. The program focuses on:

  • Building feminist leadership rooted in local and national realities
  • Strengthening cross-regional collaboration
  • Responding to rising anti-rights and anti-gender movements
  • Advancing inclusive and intersectional advocacy

In 2026, the institute will bring together 150 young women from diverse backgrounds to participate in a structured learning and advocacy journey.

Program Objectives

AAI 2026 seeks to:

  • Enhance participants’ capacity in advocacy and leadership
  • Foster intergenerational and cross-movement collaboration
  • Support national-level advocacy initiatives
  • Build a strong network of feminist leaders across regions

Thematic Focus Areas

Participants will engage with key global issues, including:

  • Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR)
  • Reproductive Justice and Bodily Autonomy
  • Gender Equality
  • Economic Justice and Human Rights Financing
  • Climate Justice
  • Digitalisation and Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • Trade, Security, and Global Systems
  • Political Equality and resistance to anti-democratic movements

Program Structure

1. Capacity Strengthening Webinars

  • A 7-week virtual learning phase
  • Includes:
    • 6 thematic webinars
    • 1 orientation session
    • Weekly office hours
    • 3 regional caucuses
  • Dates: 29 May – 10 July 2026

2. South2South Feminist Fellowship

  • Top participants advance to a fellowship phase
  • 50 fellows selected
  • Receive:
    • Technical support for advocacy projects
    • A small fellowship grant
    • Opportunity to implement national advocacy plans

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be 18–35 years old (born on or after July 1, 1992)
  • Have at least 2 years of experience in:
    • Human rights
    • Gender equality
    • SRHR advocacy
  • Be affiliated with an organization aligned with feminist and gender equality values
  • Demonstrate strong commitment to feminist principles
  • Be a national and resident of an eligible country in Africa, Asia, or the Pacific

Why You Should Apply

The AAI 2026 offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Join a global network of feminist leaders
  • Gain practical advocacy skills
  • Engage in cross-regional collaboration
  • Access mentorship and funding opportunities
  • Contribute to meaningful social and political change

The ARROW – AMwA Advocacy Institute 2026 is more than a training program—it is a platform for shaping the future of feminist advocacy across the Global South. If you are passionate about advancing gender equality and driving systemic change, this is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

Eligible Countries

Africa

Uganda, Kenya, Rwanda, Zambia, Benin, Senegal, Ghana, Morocco, Burkina Faso, Congo, Côte d’Ivoire, Niger, Togo

Asia

  • Southeast Asia: Cambodia, Myanmar, Malaysia, Indonesia, Philippines, Singapore, Laos, Vietnam
  • South Asia: Nepal, Sri Lanka, Bhutan, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Maldives
  • Central Asia: Mongolia
  • East Asia: China, Hong Kong, Japan, South Korea
  • West Asia: UAE, Qatar

Pacific

Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Cook Islands, Micronesia, Nauru, Niue, Palau, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tokelau, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu

Informational Session

Applicants are encouraged to attend a virtual information session to strengthen their applications:

  • Date: 20 April 2026
  • Time: 6:30 PM (Malaysian Time)
  • Platform: Zoom

Application Details

  • Deadline: 28 April 2026 (11:59 PM ET)
  • Language: English (French interpretation available)
  • Accessibility: Sign-language interpretation available upon request

Applicants can also access:

  • Concept Note
  • Application Guidance Documents

APPLY NOW.

Apply before 28 April 2026 and become part of a transformative movement.

Discover more Fellowship opportunity here.

The post AAI 2026 Applications Open: Join the ARROW–AMwA Feminist Advocacy Institute (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: West Africa Civil Society Institute (WACSI) Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Six-Month Civil Society & Biodiversity Research Programme for Young West African Scholars
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The West Africa Civil Society Institute (WACSI) has officially opened applications for the WACSI Research Fellowship 2026, a six-month fully supported research programme running from May to October 2026. This fellowship is designed to strengthen the research, analytical, and documentation capacities of emerging scholars while contributing to the advancement of civil society across West Africa. […]

The post West Africa Civil Society Institute (WACSI) Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Six-Month Civil Society & Biodiversity Research Programme for Young West African Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The West Africa Civil Society Institute (WACSI) has officially opened applications for the WACSI Research Fellowship 2026, a six-month fully supported research programme running from May to October 2026. This fellowship is designed to strengthen the research, analytical, and documentation capacities of emerging scholars while contributing to the advancement of civil society across West Africa.

Application Deadline: 1 May 2026
Fellowship Duration: May – October 2026 (6 months)

The programme provides young researchers with the opportunity to work closely with experienced professionals, develop high-quality publishable research, and contribute meaningfully to civil society discourse in the region.

About WACSI

The West Africa Civil Society Institute (WACSI) was established to reinforce institutional, operational, and knowledge capacities of civil society organisations (CSOs) in West Africa. The Institute serves as a regional resource hub for training, research, documentation, policy dialogue, and experience sharing.

Through its research fellowship, WACSI identifies and nurtures young researchers, equipping them with skills that strengthen both academia and civil society ecosystems.

Fellowship Focus for 2026

The 2026 fellowship prioritises the documentation of indigenous knowledge within the biodiversity and conservation sector. Fellows will examine how civil society organisations can incorporate traditional knowledge systems to enhance environmental advocacy and climate action.

Indigenous knowledge refers to traditional systems of practices, beliefs, innovations, and experiences developed and transmitted across generations within communities. In the biodiversity and conservation space, such knowledge can provide valuable insight into sustainable environmental management and climate resilience.

Fellows will explore intersections between academia and CSOs working in biodiversity and conservation while documenting replicable best practices.

Research Areas of Interest

Applicants are encouraged to submit proposals related to biodiversity and conservation, including but not limited to:

  • Leadership and governance in civil society organisations

  • Technology for development

  • Resource mobilisation strategies

  • Civil society sustainability

  • Working practices and internal systems

  • Human resource management

  • Enabling environments for civil society

  • Community engagement strategies

  • Equitable partnerships between West African CSOs and international donors

  • Capacity strengthening for civil society

Applicants must demonstrate a strong connection between their topic and biodiversity and conservation.

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship is open to young researchers from:

  • Ghana

  • Guinea Conakry

  • Liberia

  • Nigeria

  • Senegal

Applicants must:

  • Demonstrate strong analytical and independent research skills

  • Show clear relevance of their proposed topic to biodiversity and conservation

  • Be fluent in English and/or French

  • Be willing to commit to the full six-month fellowship

Current staff of organisations participating in biodiversity capacity strengthening programmes are encouraged to apply. Independent researchers are also welcome.

Fellowship Benefits

Participants will receive:

Monthly Stipend
Research Allowance

Mentorship
Dedicated mentors will guide fellows throughout the programme.

Publication Opportunities

  • At least one publishable research report or policy paper

  • Opportunity to produce an Issue Paper, Op-Ed, blog article, or WACSeries publication

  • Publication on WACSI’s globally recognised platform

Professional Development

  • Enhanced research and analytical skills

  • Networking opportunities within the civil society ecosystem

  • Exposure to regional and international audiences

Access to Resources

  • Free access to WACSI publications

Fellowship Deliverables

Selected fellows must:

  • Contribute to ongoing or commissioned research by WACSI

  • Produce at least one publishable article on best practices or case studies in biodiversity and conservation

  • Develop at least one WACSeries publication

Why Apply?

The WACSI Research Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Strengthen research capabilities

  • Contribute to biodiversity and climate advocacy

  • Gain mentorship from experienced professionals

  • Publish research on a respected regional platform

  • Build a career within civil society and policy research

This fellowship bridges academia and civil society, empowering young researchers to influence policy and practice in West Africa.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Motivation letter (including duration and theme of interest)

  • Curriculum vitae

  • One-page synopsis outlining proposed research theme and methodology

  • Sample authored article or research paper

  • Evidence of previous publications (if available)

Applications must be submitted via email to: research@wacsi.org and recruitment@wacsi.org

All applications must be received by 1 May 2026.

Testimonials from Past Fellows

Previous fellows highlight the programme’s transformative impact on their research confidence, mentorship experience, and professional growth. Participants report improved qualitative research skills, policy writing experience, and valuable exposure to new thematic areas such as social impact investment.

Learn more about the WACSI Research Fellowship HERE.

Click HERE to discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY.

The post West Africa Civil Society Institute (WACSI) Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Six-Month Civil Society & Biodiversity Research Programme for Young West African Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Foundation Grant 2026: Complete Application Guide, Timeline, and Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits in the Global South
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The International Foundation Grant 2026 Program is now officially announced, offering nonprofit organizations across the Global South a valuable opportunity to secure funding for impactful projects in agriculture, education, environment, and health. This grant program is highly competitive and structured in multiple stages, requiring organizations to carefully follow the application process and timeline. With a […]

The post International Foundation Grant 2026: Complete Application Guide, Timeline, and Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits in the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Foundation Grant 2026 Program is now officially announced, offering nonprofit organizations across the Global South a valuable opportunity to secure funding for impactful projects in agriculture, education, environment, and health.

This grant program is highly competitive and structured in multiple stages, requiring organizations to carefully follow the application process and timeline. With a strict limit on submissions and an early opening cycle for 2026, applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and apply as early as possible.

About the International Foundation Grant Program

The International Foundation supports nonprofit organizations that are working to create sustainable and measurable impact in underserved communities. The foundation prioritizes projects that address pressing global challenges and contribute to long-term development outcomes.

Key Focus Areas

Organizations applying for this grant must align their projects with one or more of the following sectors:

  • Agriculture – Sustainable farming, food security, and rural development
  • Education – Access to quality education and capacity building
  • Environment – Climate action, conservation, and natural resource management
  • Health – Public health, access to healthcare, and community well-being

2026 Grant Timeline: Important Dates

The 2026 grant cycle follows a structured timeline with multiple phases:

Information Session

  • April 8, 2026: Webinar introducing the grant program

Eligibility & Letter of Inquiry (LOI)

  • April 15 – April 29, 2026: Eligibility evaluation and LOI submission window
  • Important: Portal closes once 150 LOIs are submitted (even before April 29)

Advancement Notification

  • May 7, 2026: Applicants notified if selected for the next stage

Full Application Stage

  • May 8, 2026: Application portal opens
  • June 1, 2026: Application deadline

Awarding Phase

  • August – September 2026: Award notifications
  • September – October 2026: Grant disbursement

Grant Implementation & Reporting

  • June 2027: Mid-term report due
  • December 31, 2027: Grant period ends
  • January 31, 2028: Final report submission deadline

Step-by-Step Application Process

The International Foundation has designed a multi-stage selection process to ensure that only the most impactful and well-prepared projects receive funding.

Step 1: Information Session

Organizations are encouraged to attend the virtual webinar on April 8, 2026, which provides:

  • Overview of the grant program
  • Eligibility requirements
  • Application guidance

This session is highly recommended for first-time applicants.

Step 2: Eligibility Evaluation

The application process begins with an online eligibility evaluation, opening on April 15, 2026.

Organizations must:

  • Complete a short online form
  • Demonstrate alignment with eligibility criteria

If eligible, applicants will be invited to submit a Letter of Inquiry (LOI).

Important Note:
The portal will close either on April 29, 2026 or when 150 LOIs are submitted—whichever comes first. Early submission is critical.

Step 3: Letter of Inquiry (LOI)

The LOI is a short preliminary proposal submitted in a Q&A format, covering:

  • Overview of the organization
  • Description of the proposed project
  • Intended use of funds

LOIs are evaluated based on:

  • Alignment with the foundation’s priorities
  • Geographic focus
  • Potential impact

Applicants will be notified by May 7, 2026 if they are selected to proceed.

Step 4: Full Proposal Submission

Selected organizations will gain access to the Grant Application Portal, opening on May 8, 2026.

The full proposal requires:

  • Detailed project description
  • Expected outcomes and impact
  • Organizational capacity
  • Financial documentation

The submission deadline is June 1, 2026.

Step 5: Review and Final Decision

All applications are reviewed by:

  • The International Foundation’s Grants Committee
  • The Board of Directors for final approval

This ensures a rigorous and transparent selection process.

Grant Terms and Reporting Requirements

Organizations selected for funding must adhere to a structured reporting framework:

  • Submit a mid-term report by June 2027
  • Complete the project by December 31, 2027
  • Submit a final report by January 31, 2028

Important Conditions to Note

  • Only one application per organization is allowed per cycle
  • Organizations with previous funding must have fully completed prior grants before reapplying
  • Applications are highly competitive due to limited LOI slots

Why You Should Apply

The International Foundation Grant is an excellent opportunity for nonprofit organizations to:

  • Secure funding for impactful development projects
  • Scale existing initiatives
  • Strengthen organizational capacity
  • Gain recognition from an international funding body

With limited application slots and high demand, preparation and early submission are key to success.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post International Foundation Grant 2026: Complete Application Guide, Timeline, and Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits in the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Consultant Opportunity: Generation Unlimited – UNICEF South Africa
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
UNICEF South Africa is hiring a consultant to support its Generation Unlimited initiative, which aims to connect 1.5 million young people to economic opportunities by 2030. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about youth empowerment, innovation, and impact-driven work. About the Role The selected consultant will support efforts to expand access to economic […]

The post Consultant Opportunity: Generation Unlimited – UNICEF South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
UNICEF South Africa is hiring a consultant to support its Generation Unlimited initiative, which aims to connect 1.5 million young people to economic opportunities by 2030.

This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about youth empowerment, innovation, and impact-driven work.

About the Role

The selected consultant will support efforts to expand access to economic opportunities for young people across South Africa.

This includes contributing to:

Program strategy and implementation
Innovative solutions for youth employment
Partnership development and stakeholder engagement
Monitoring and impact-driven initiatives

Who Should Apply

This role is ideal for individuals who:

Have a strong passion for youth development
Bring innovative thinking and problem-solving skills
Have experience in program management, partnerships, or social impact work
Are interested in working within an international development context

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Generation Unlimited initiative is a global effort to ensure that young people have access to education, employment, and entrepreneurship opportunities.

By joining this work, you contribute directly to shaping the future of youth livelihoods and economic inclusion.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Job Opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Consultant Opportunity: Generation Unlimited – UNICEF South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rise on Boards Program – Applications Open
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Rise on Boards Program by Fora Network for Change, a leadership initiative designed to equip young women and gender-marginalized youth with the skills and confidence to serve on boards and lead effectively. This global program is open to both national and international applicants, making it accessible no matter where […]

The post Rise on Boards Program – Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Rise on Boards Program by Fora Network for Change, a leadership initiative designed to equip young women and gender-marginalized youth with the skills and confidence to serve on boards and lead effectively.

This global program is open to both national and international applicants, making it accessible no matter where you are in the world.

About the Program

The Rise on Boards stream provides a structured learning experience focused on governance, leadership, and decision-making.

Participants will engage in:

Live training sessions
Applied, hands-on learning
Mentorship from experienced leaders

What You’ll Gain

Knowledge of governance and board readiness
Confidence navigating power and accountability
Strategic decision-making skills
Access to a global network of peers and mentors

Who Should Apply

Young women and gender-marginalized youth aged 18–29
Individuals passionate about community engagement and leadership
Applicants interested in training delivered in English or French

Key Details

Deadline: 30 April 2026 (11:59 PM EST)
Eligibility: Open globally
Program Type: Leadership / Training Program

Why This Opportunity Matters

The program is designed to close leadership gaps by empowering underrepresented voices to take up space in governance and decision-making roles across sectors.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here

For more Training opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Rise on Boards Program – Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
